title: Workspace manifest
description: Formal schema for a workspace manifest per D7 (workspace kind). The workspace = manifest + state; this schema covers the *manifest* (declarative). Runtime state (events, work-units, scope) is event-sourced and validated per its own kinds' schemas. Notation: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 per D28. Layer-3 formal schema per D11. Cross-kind referential integrity is handled by the framework conformance validator at boot per D29 + D30 + D32.

id.description: Stable, unique workspace identifier (D7 §1). Persists across full lifecycle. Instance-identifier per D34.

description.description: Optional human-readable description; not load-bearing at framework-core.

composition.description: Declarative bindings the workspace composes (D7 §2). Validated at boot per D29 validation flow.

extensions.description: Extensions this workspace references, by (id, version-range). Resolved at boot per D29; version-conflict resolution per D33.

shape.description: Exactly 1 shape per workspace (D7 §2). Per D31, no inheritance. References a shape provision by fully-qualified <ext-id>:<id> per D29.

substrate-bindings.description: 1+ substrate bindings (D7 §2 + §4). Each binding selects exactly one runtime-shape. Multiple bindings of the same substrate (different runtime-shapes) allowed.

items.description: Per D34 §6.4: at least one of `provision` or `required-capabilities` must be supplied (specific identity vs. capability-based vs. mixed per D12). A binding declaring neither would be unbootable.

binding-id.description: Stable id within this workspace; referenced by agent-actors per D9 + D22.

actors.description: 1+ actors (D7 §2; D9). Items validate against actor.schema.json. Note: composition is mutable per D7 §4 — runtime-added agent-actors (e.g., sub-agents per D19) join the workspace's actor registry via composition-change events; per-event identity resolution per D34 §6.2 uses the current registry, not just the manifest snapshot.

adapter-bindings.description: 0+ adapter bindings (D7 §2). Each references an adapter provision by fully-qualified id and supplies binding-time configuration.

specialist-bindings.description: 0+ specialist bindings (D7 §2; D19). binding-id is the workspace-scoped identifier referenced by work-unit.contributing-specialists[] per D34 §1.2.
